article.title: Firefox 12.58% for Desktop Browser Market Share in North America June 2026

summary: In June 2026, Firefox secured a 12.58% share of the desktop browser market in North America, reflecting its continued relevance despite intense competition. This metric highlights Firefox's sustained user base amid dominance by browsers like Chrome, underscoring the importance of privacy and open-source values in user choice.
keyPoints: Firefox held 12.58% of the North American desktop browser market in June 2026.
The data reflects ongoing competition with dominant browsers such as Chrome and Edge.
Firefox's market share indicates steady user loyalty driven by privacy and customization features.
Statcounter's data is widely used by over one million professionals for market insights.
Browser market share trends influence web development, SEO strategies, and digital marketing.

rewrite.title: Firefox Holds 12.58% Desktop Share in North America
rewrite.body:
As of June 2026, Firefox commands a notable 12.58% share of the desktop browser market in North America. This figure underscores Firefox's resilience in a landscape heavily dominated by Google Chrome and Microsoft's Edge. Despite the competitive pressure, Firefox maintains a loyal user base attracted by its commitment to privacy, open-source development, and extensive customization options.

The browser market share data, sourced from Statcounter, is a critical barometer for web developers, marketers, and SEO professionals who tailor their strategies based on user preferences. Firefox's sustained presence at over 12% signals that a significant segment of users prioritize security and transparency, influencing how websites are optimized and how digital campaigns are designed.

This market share also has second-order effects on the broader tech ecosystem, encouraging innovation in privacy features and standards compliance. As browsers compete not only on speed and compatibility but also on data protection, Firefox's position helps keep these issues at the forefront of browser development discussions.

Overall, Firefox's 12.58% desktop market share in North America reflects a balanced browser ecosystem where user choice extends beyond the dominant players, shaping the future of web experience and digital privacy norms.
